comment_text.sn35.70:1.3: “Apparent in this very life” (sandiṭṭhika) is one of the fundamental characteristics of the Dhamma, which are recited in the Pali itipi so formula to this day.

root_text.sn35.70:1.1: Atha kho āyasmā upavāṇo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami …pe…
root_text.sn35.70:1.2: ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā upavāṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.70:1.4: Kittāvatā nu kho, bhante, sandiṭṭhiko dhammo hoti, akāliko ehipassiko opaneyyiko paccattaṁ veditabbo viññūhī”ti?
root_text.sn35.70:2.1: “Idha pana, upavāṇa, bhikkhu cakkhunā rūpaṁ disvā rūpappaṭisaṁvedī ca hoti rūparāgappaṭisaṁvedī ca.
root_text.sn35.70:2.2: Santañca ajjhattaṁ rūpesu rāgaṁ ‘atthi me ajjhattaṁ rūpesu rāgo’ti pajānāti.
root_text.sn35.70:2.3: Yaṁ taṁ, upavāṇa, bhikkhu cakkhunā rūpaṁ disvā rūpappaṭisaṁvedī ca hoti rūparāgappaṭisaṁvedī ca.
root_text.sn35.70:2.4: Santañca ajjhattaṁ rūpesu rāgaṁ ‘atthi me ajjhattaṁ rūpesu rāgo’ti pajānāti.
root_text.sn35.70:2.5: Evampi kho, upavāṇa, sandiṭṭhiko dhammo hoti akāliko ehipassiko opaneyyiko paccattaṁ veditabbo viññūhi …pe….
root_text.sn35.70:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, upavāṇa, bhikkhu jivhāya rasaṁ sāyitvā rasappaṭisaṁvedī ca hoti rasarāgappaṭisaṁvedī ca.
root_text.sn35.70:3.2: Santañca ajjhattaṁ rasesu rāgaṁ ‘atthi me ajjhattaṁ rasesu rāgo’ti pajānāti.
root_text.sn35.70:3.3: Yaṁ taṁ, upavāṇa, bhikkhu jivhāya rasaṁ sāyitvā rasappaṭisaṁvedī ca hoti rasarāgappaṭisaṁvedī ca.
root_text.sn35.70:3.4: Santañca ajjhattaṁ rasesu rāgaṁ ‘atthi me ajjhattaṁ rasesu rāgo’ti pajānāti. Evampi kho, upavāṇa, sandiṭṭhiko dhammo hoti akāliko ehipassiko opaneyyiko paccattaṁ veditabbo viññūhi …pe….
root_text.sn35.70:4.1: Puna caparaṁ, upavāṇa, bhikkhu manasā dhammaṁ viññāya dhammappaṭisaṁvedī ca hoti dhammarāgappaṭisaṁvedī ca.
root_text.sn35.70:4.2: Santañca ajjhattaṁ dhammesu rāgaṁ ‘atthi me ajjhattaṁ dhammesu rāgo’ti pajānāti.
root_text.sn35.70:4.3: Yaṁ taṁ, upavāṇa, bhikkhu manasā dhammaṁ viññāya dhammappaṭisaṁvedī ca hoti dhammarāgappaṭisaṁvedī ca.
root_text.sn35.70:4.4: Santañca ajjhattaṁ dhammesu rāgaṁ ‘atthi me ajjhattaṁ dhammesu rāgo’ti pajānāti.
root_text.sn35.70:4.5: Evampi kho, upavāṇa, sandiṭṭhiko dhammo hoti …pe… paccattaṁ veditabbo viññūhi …pe….
root_text.sn35.70:5.1: Idha pana, upavāṇa, bhikkhu cakkhunā rūpaṁ disvā rūpappaṭisaṁvedī ca hoti, no ca rūparāgappaṭisaṁvedī.
root_text.sn35.70:5.2: Asantañca ajjhattaṁ rūpesu rāgaṁ ‘natthi me ajjhattaṁ rūpesu rāgo’ti pajānāti.
root_text.sn35.70:5.3: Yaṁ taṁ, upavāṇa, bhikkhu cakkhunā rūpaṁ disvā rūpappaṭisaṁvedīhi kho hoti, no ca rūparāgappaṭisaṁvedī.
root_text.sn35.70:5.4: Asantañca ajjhattaṁ rūpesu rāgaṁ ‘natthi me ajjhattaṁ rūpesu rāgo’ti pajānāti.
root_text.sn35.70:5.5: Evampi kho, upavāṇa, sandiṭṭhiko dhammo hoti, akāliko ehipassiko opaneyyiko paccattaṁ veditabbo viññūhi …pe….
root_text.sn35.70:6.1: Puna caparaṁ, upavāṇa, bhikkhu jivhāya rasaṁ sāyitvā rasappaṭisaṁvedīhi kho hoti, no ca rasarāgappaṭisaṁvedī. Asantañca ajjhattaṁ rasesu rāgaṁ ‘natthi me ajjhattaṁ rasesu rāgo’ti pajānāti …pe….
root_text.sn35.70:7.1: Puna caparaṁ, upavāṇa, bhikkhu manasā dhammaṁ viññāya dhammappaṭisaṁvedīhi kho hoti, no ca dhammarāgappaṭisaṁvedī.
root_text.sn35.70:7.2: Asantañca ajjhattaṁ dhammesu rāgaṁ ‘natthi me ajjhattaṁ dhammesu rāgo’ti pajānāti.
root_text.sn35.70:7.3: Yaṁ taṁ, upavāṇa, bhikkhu manasā dhammaṁ viññāya dhammappaṭisaṁvedīhi kho hoti, no ca dhammarāgappaṭisaṁvedī.
root_text.sn35.70:7.4: Asantañca ajjhattaṁ dhammesu rāgaṁ ‘natthi me ajjhattaṁ dhammesu rāgo’ti pajānāti.
root_text.sn35.70:7.5: Evampi kho, upavāṇa, sandiṭṭhiko dhammo hoti, akāliko ehipassiko opaneyyiko paccattaṁ veditabbo viññūhī”ti.

translation_text.sn35.70:0.3: Upavāṇa on What is Apparent in the Present Life
translation_text.sn35.70:1.1: Then Venerable Upavāṇa went up to the Buddha …
translation_text.sn35.70:1.3: “Sir, they speak of ‘a teaching apparent in the present life’.
translation_text.sn35.70:1.4: In what way is the teaching apparent in the present life, immediately effective, inviting inspection, relevant, so that sensible people can know it for themselves?”
translation_text.sn35.70:2.1: “Upavāṇa, take a mendicant who sees a sight with their eyes. They experience both the sight and the desire for the sight.
translation_text.sn35.70:2.2: There is desire for sights in them, and they understand that.
translation_text.sn35.70:2.5: this is how the teaching is apparent in the present life, immediately effective, inviting inspection, relevant, so that sensible people can know it for themselves.
translation_text.sn35.70:3.1: Next, take a mendicant who hears … smells … tastes … touches …
translation_text.sn35.70:4.1: Next, take a mendicant who knows an idea with their mind. They experience both the idea and the desire for the idea.
translation_text.sn35.70:4.2: There is desire for ideas in them, and they understand that.
translation_text.sn35.70:4.5: this is how the teaching is apparent in the present life, immediately effective, inviting inspection, relevant, so that sensible people can know it for themselves.
translation_text.sn35.70:5.1: Take a mendicant who sees a sight with their eyes. They experience the sight but no desire for the sight.
translation_text.sn35.70:5.2: There is no desire for sights in them, and they understand that.
translation_text.sn35.70:5.5: this is how the teaching is apparent in the present life, immediately effective, inviting inspection, relevant, so that sensible people can know it for themselves.
translation_text.sn35.70:6.1: Next, take a mendicant who hears … smells … tastes … touches …
translation_text.sn35.70:7.3: Next, take a mendicant who knows an idea with their mind. They experience the idea but no desire for the idea.
translation_text.sn35.70:7.4: There is no desire for ideas in them, and they understand that.
translation_text.sn35.70:7.5: Since this is so, this is how the teaching is apparent in the present life, immediately effective, inviting inspection, relevant, so that sensible people can know it for themselves.”
